resources.string: Helps you avoid falling into Instagram Reels and YouTube Shorts. When it detects you opened them, it closes that section so you can keep doing what you were doing. Nothing is sent off the device.
Tap your mascot to say hi
Without this, Basta! can\'t detect Reels. Tap and enable Basta! in the list.
Without this, the system can kill the service after a few hours.
We detected you disabled the protection.

plurals.name: external_disable_dialog_body
plurals.item: You disabled the protection. You lost your streak of %1$d day and your %2$s went back to an egg.
You disabled the protection. You lost your streak of %1$d days and your %2$s went back to an egg.

resources.string: Tap your mascot to say hi
Turn on the service to start your streak
Start a new streak — the day counts when midnight hits
Ready to graduate — open the app to confirm
Tomorrow you can save your %1$s in the inventory
When you open Reels (Instagram) or Shorts (YouTube), Basta! detects the screen and presses Back for you. You leave before the scroll starts. The rest of the app is untouched.
Each day with the service active adds a day to your streak. If you disable it or skip a full day, it goes back to 0. Your all-time record is kept even if you break the streak.

The mascot evolves with you:
 · Day 0 → Egg
 · Day 3 → Cracking
 · Day 8 → Hatchling
 · Day 21 → Adult

When you reach day 21, your mascot graduates: it\'s saved in your inventory and a new egg of a different species appears. There are 5 species in total (Classic, Dragon, Turtle, Wolf, Owl). The reset only happens at graduation — if you break the streak before, you keep the same species.
The paw icon 🐾 in the bottom bar opens your collection. You\'ll see the mascots you\'ve graduated, the ones you still need, and how many days the active one has been with you.
For it to work: the accessibility service must be on and the app must be excluded from battery optimization. If Android kills the service, we\'ll let you know when you come back.
After %1$d days, it\'s heading off. It\'ll be saved in your inventory.
A new egg of another species is waiting.
I went %1$d days without falling into Reels with my %2$s. Using Basta! — the app that pulls you out of short video.
You\'ll lose the streak and your mascot will go back to an egg.
" will go back to an egg."
No history yet. Come back tomorrow — data builds up.
It\'ll start counting after your first block
1 ten-minute break without losing the streak. For the days life asks for something else.
Reach day 21 with the streak active to graduate a mascot.
You\'ve collected all 5 species. New ones can repeat.
You haven\'t graduated any mascot yet
Your first graduation arrives at day 21 of an active streak.
%1$s locked. Tap to unlock with Pro.
95 min/day. That\'s the average we lose on Reels and Shorts. Almost an hour and a half. Every day.\n\nWhen you go in, this app pulls you out. No motivational coaches, no lectures.
Every day with Basta! active, your mascot grows. At day 21 it graduates and gets saved in your inventory, and an egg of a different species appears. There are 5 to collect.
It\'s the only way Android can detect Reels. It only reads screen identifiers, never the content of your messages.
And tell the battery not to kill us
Without this, the system kills the service in hours and Basta! is gone.
Reels and Shorts close on their own
Exclude Basta! from battery optimization so the system can\'t stop the service
Enable the accessibility service to start
Turn your blocked apps back on to resume protection

Basta! exists because no one else is on your side when the algorithm starts pulling. We built it small, local, and silent — no metrics on you, no upselling at every screen, no infinite scroll of its own. It does one job: when you fall into Reels or Shorts, it pulls you out before you notice the hour is gone. The rest is yours.
Not excluded — the system can kill the service
Opens accessibility settings to turn off the service. You\'ll break your streak and your mascot will go back to an egg.
You have access to all advanced options
If you already paid Pro on another device or reinstall
Reopens the onboarding step by step
What data the app touches — and what it doesn\'t

plurals.name: app_disable_confirm_body
plurals.item: If you turn it off, your streak of %2$d day will break and your mascot will go back to an egg.
If you turn it off, your streak of %2$d days will break and your mascot will go back to an egg.

resources.string: The streak doesn\'t break. The mascot dozes off. We\'ll let you know when it\'s back.
Reclaim what scrolling stole from you.
One 10-min daily break — streak doesn\'t break, mascot doesn\'t die
Allow Reels sent from friends\' DMs
No algorithm. No tracking. Nobody living off your attention.
Without Pro, the app still blocks Reels and Shorts. Pro is for those who want finer control over what slips through.
We don\'t sell your data. We don\'t collect it.
Your progress travels with you via Google Drive (encrypted by Google).
Reclaim what scrolling stole from you.
Three more species. Real breaks. The whole bestiary.
Managed by Google Play. Cancel anytime in the Play Store.
Reels sent by friends in direct messages are not blocked.
By default they\'re not blocked. Enable it if you want them out too.
Battery: the system can kill the service ⚠
On your phone (%1$s), besides the battery exemption go to Settings → Apps → Basta! → Auto-launch and enable it. Otherwise the system will kill the background service.

string-array.name: tips_quotes
string-array.item: The average user spends 95 min/day on Reels and Shorts. Almost a movie.
Your brain needs 23 min to recover focus after each distraction.
Reels plays 30 videos in 5 min. 30 micro-doses of dopamine.
Infinite scroll is designed so your brain doesn\'t decide to stop.
1 hour of screen before sleep = 22 min less deep sleep.
70% of users lose track of time on Reels.
TikTok hooks you in 8 min of use. Faster than coffee.
An average Reels session lasts 24 min. Almost half an episode of something good.
The younger the user, the more time on short video. Hard to see it in grandparents.
Every like you get on Reels isn\'t yours. It\'s the algorithm deciding for you.
Teens with +3h/day on short video have 2x more anxiety symptoms.
60% of people check their phone within 10 min of waking up.
We spend on average 3h 15min/day on the phone. That\'s 49 full days a year.
Blue light from the screen delays melatonin release by 1.5 hours.
5 min of Reels = 5 min reading. When did you last finish a whole book?
If you have 20 min, go for a walk. You don\'t see the sun while doomscrolling.
Call a friend. One conversation beats 100 reels together.
Being bored is the signal that your brain is about to create something. Don\'t shut it off.
Cook something simple instead of scrolling. You eat better and get what you wanted.
10 min of stretching when waking up > 10 min of Reels in bed.
Write down 3 things you want to do today. Start with one, without thinking.
Step out on the balcony for 2 min. The screen isn\'t going anywhere.
20 squats now give you more energy than 20 reels.
Writing a long message to someone who matters hits harder than any scroll.
A coffee with someone in person beats a week of stories.
If you\'re bored, look out the window for 30 seconds. Seriously.
Listen to a song with eyes closed. Plenty as a pause.
Play a song and dance for 3 minutes. Your body thanks you.
30 seconds of cold shower. Resets you better than 50 reels.
Water a plant. Brings the brain to something slow and alive.
The first scroll isn\'t the problem. Number 50 is.
You don\'t have to be productive all the time. A bit of boredom is fine.
The hard part isn\'t quitting Reels. It\'s not opening them without thinking.
The algorithm remembers you every second. How often do you remember the algorithm?
Every time this app pulls you out, you gain time you didn\'t see leaving.
Your free time isn\'t \'dead time\'. It\'s some of the few moments that are yours.
Choose your distractions on purpose. The ones that surprise you hurt the most.
If you open Reels to \'rest\', you end up more tired. Confirmed.
Real curiosity lasts hours. Reels\' curiosity, 8 seconds.
Close your eyes for 10 seconds. That\'s more rest than half an hour of scroll.
Good things take time. Reels arrive in 15 seconds and leave just as fast.
Attention is the scarcest resource of the century. And you give it away for free.
Question: whose was the last reel you saw? Exactly.
Before grabbing the phone, breathe three times. Sometimes the urge passes.
